activities_projects.title: Timeline of Social Reformers
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of major social reformers in improving the condition of women and lower castes
steps: List important reformers mentioned in the chapter
Find the years in which they worked
Create a timeline on chart paper
Place pictures or names of reformers at correct years
Explain one reform done by each reformer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn about major reformers and their contributions to social change
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct timeline and explanation of reforms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Social Reform
activities_projects.objective: Understand different viewpoints about social reforms in the 19th century
steps: Divide students into small groups
Assign each group a reformer such as Raja Rammohan Roy or Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar
Prepare short dialogues explaining their reforms
Perform the role play in front of the class
Discuss the importance of their work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how reformers worked to change social practices
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and understanding of reform movements (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poster on Women’s Education
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of women's education in social reform
steps: Discuss how women’s education was restricted in the past
List reasons why education is important for women
Design a poster promoting girls’ education
Add slogans supporting equal education
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the value of education in improving women's status

activities_projects.title: Debate on Child Marriage
activities_projects.objective: Understand social problems and reform movements against child marriage
steps: Divide the class into two groups
Assign one group to discuss problems of child marriage
Let the other group discuss reforms against child marriage
Conduct a short classroom debate
Summarize the conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand why social reform laws were needed
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and reasoning in debate (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biography Study of Reformers
activities_projects.objective: Learn about important reformers who worked for equality
steps: Choose a reformer mentioned in the chapter
Read about their life and contributions
Write a short biography in your notebook
Share the findings with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain knowledge about contributions of social reformers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of biography and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Understanding Caste Inequality
activities_projects.objective: Understand discrimination faced by lower caste communities
steps: Read the section about caste discrimination
List examples of social inequality mentioned in the chapter
Write them on chart paper
Discuss how reform movements tried to remove discrimination
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand caste-based inequalities and reform efforts
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of caste discrimination and reforms (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Newspapers and Social Reform
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of print culture in spreading reform ideas
steps: Discuss how newspapers spread reform ideas
Find an article related to social equality or education
Read and summarize the article
Explain how newspapers influence public opinion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how print media helped spread reform movements

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Past and Present
activities_projects.objective: Compare the status of women in the past and present
steps: List conditions of women in the 19th century
List opportunities available for women today
Create a comparison table
Discuss improvements and remaining challenges
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand social changes over time
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion on Equality
activities_projects.objective: Promote awareness about equality and social justice
steps: Form small groups
Discuss the importance of equality in society
Share examples of fairness and discrimination
Present group ideas to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness about equality and social responsibility
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Essay on Social Reform
activities_projects.objective: Develop writing skills while understanding social reform movements
steps: Review the chapter content
Write an essay on 'Importance of Social Reform in India'
Include examples of reformers and their work
Share the essay with the teacher
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the impact of reform movements in Indian society
